Package calendar.model

Interface CalendarInterface

  • All Known Implementing Classes:
    Calendar, ForthMockModel
    public interface CalendarInterface
    This interface contains necessary operations that a calendar should support.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      void convertTimezone​(java.time.ZoneId oldZone, java.time.ZoneId newZone)
      Convert events in the calendar to a new timezone.

      To create an event series with necessary subject, startDateTime, endDateTime and optional properties.

      To create a single event with necessary subject, startDateTime, endDateTime and optional properties.

      To edit an event series given subject, startDateTime and endDateTime.

      To edit a single event given subject, startDateTime and endDateTime.

      Exports all events in the calendar to a CSV formatted string.

      Exports all events in the calendar to iCal format.

      Get all events in the calendar.

      Retrieves all events within the given time range.

      Get the event series with given seriesId.

      Get all events that take place on the given date.

      To get a single event with subject, startDateTime and optional properties given.
      UserStatus getUserStatus​(java.time.LocalDateTime queryTime)
      Show UserStatus on a given date and/or time.

    • Method Detail

      • createSingleEvent

        Event createSingleEvent​(EventContext context,
                        java.lang.String seriesId)
                 throws java.lang.IllegalArgumentException
        To create a single event with necessary subject, startDateTime, endDateTime and optional properties. If not specify endDateTime, the event will be an all-day event with default time range from 8am to 5pm. A single event may span across days. Two events cannot have the same subject, start date/time and end date/time.
        Parameters:
        context - the parameter object to pass event properties
        seriesId - null by default; if this event is a single independent event, then null; if belongs to a series, then not null
        Returns:
        the single event that is created; if fail to create, return null
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if inputs are not illegally formatted

      • createEventSeries

        EventSeries createEventSeries​(EventContext context,
                              java.lang.String weekdays,
                              int repeatTimes,
                              java.lang.String seriesEndDateTime)
                       throws java.lang.IllegalArgumentException
        To create an event series with necessary subject, startDateTime, endDateTime and optional properties. All events in an event series are required to have the same start time. An event series must specify its occurrences in which weekdays, and then specify repeatTimes or until a specific seriesEndDateTime. A single event in a series can only span one day (it must start and finish on the same day). Two events in the calendar cannot have the same subject, start date/time and end date/time.
        Parameters:
        context - the parameter object to pass event properties
        weekdays - (optional) a sequence of characters where each character denotes a day of the event's occurrence e.g., MRU. 'M' is Monday, 'T' is Tuesday, 'W' is Wednesday, 'R' is Thursday, 'F' is Friday, 'S' is Saturday, and 'U' is Sunday
        repeatTimes - (optional) the int that denotes occurrences of the event in a week
        seriesEndDateTime - (optional) the end date and/or time of the series
        Returns:
        the event series that is created; if fail to create, return null
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if inputs are not illegally formatted

      • getSingleEvent

        Event getSingleEvent​(java.lang.String subject,
                     java.lang.String startDateTime,
                     java.lang.String endDateTime)
              throws java.lang.IllegalArgumentException
        To get a single event with subject, startDateTime and optional properties given.
        Parameters:
        subject - the theme of the event series on calendar (must specify)
        startDateTime - the start date and/or time of the event series (must specify)
        endDateTime - the end date and/or time of the event series (must specify) if input is null, search for an all-day event
        Returns:
        the single event that is created; if fail to create, return null
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if inputs are not illegally formatted

      • editSingleEvent

        Event editSingleEvent​(java.lang.String subject,
                      java.lang.String startDateTime,
                      java.lang.String endDateTime,
                      EventContext newContext)
               throws java.lang.IllegalArgumentException
        To edit a single event given subject, startDateTime and endDateTime. Error will show if the event to be edited does not exist.
        Parameters:
        subject - the theme of the event to be edited on calendar
        startDateTime - the start date and/or time of the event to be edited
        endDateTime - the end date and/or time of the event to be edited
        newContext - the parameter object to pass new event properties
        Returns:
        the event that has been updated; null if edition fails
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if inputs are not illegally formatted

      • editEventSeries

        EventSeries editEventSeries​(java.lang.String subject,
                            java.lang.String startDateTime,
                            java.lang.String endDateTime,
                            EventContext newContext)
                     throws java.lang.IllegalArgumentException
        To edit an event series given subject, startDateTime and endDateTime. Error will show if the event series to be edited does not exist.
        Parameters:
        subject - the theme of the event series to be edited on calendar
        startDateTime - the start date and/or time of the event series to be edited
        endDateTime - the end date and/or time of the event series to be edited
        newContext - the parameter object to pass new event properties
        Returns:
        the event series that has been updated; null if edition fails
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if inputs are not illegally formatted

      • getEventsOnDate

        java.util.List<Event> getEventsOnDate​(java.time.LocalDate date)
        Get all events that take place on the given date.
        Parameters:
        date - the date to query
        Returns:
        a List of Event objects scheduled on the date, sorted by start time

      • getEventsBetween

        java.util.List<Event> getEventsBetween​(java.time.LocalDateTime start,
                                       java.time.LocalDateTime end)
        Retrieves all events within the given time range.
        Parameters:
        start - the start date & time of the query range
        end - the end date & time of the query range
        Returns:
        a List of Event objects within the range, sorted by start time

      • getUserStatus

        UserStatus getUserStatus​(java.time.LocalDateTime queryTime)
        Show UserStatus on a given date and/or time.
        Parameters:
        queryTime - the given query time
        Returns:
        If there exists event on the given date and/or time, return BUSY; else, AVAILABLE

      • exportToCsv

        java.lang.String exportToCsv()
        Exports all events in the calendar to a CSV formatted string.
        Returns:
        a String representing the full CSV content, including headers.

      • exportToIcal

        java.lang.String exportToIcal​(java.lang.String calendarName,
                              java.time.ZoneId timezone)
        Exports all events in the calendar to iCal format.
        Parameters:
        calendarName - the name of the calendar
        timezone - the timezone of the calendar
        Returns:
        an iCal formatted string

      • getEvents

        java.util.Collection<Event> getEvents()
        Get all events in the calendar.
        Returns:
        the collection of events.

      • getEventSeries

        EventSeries getEventSeries​(java.lang.String seriesId)
        Get the event series with given seriesId.
        Parameters:
        seriesId - the seriesId to identify an event series
        Returns:
        the event series with given seriesId

      • convertTimezone

        void convertTimezone​(java.time.ZoneId oldZone,
                     java.time.ZoneId newZone)
        Convert events in the calendar to a new timezone.
        Parameters:
        oldZone - the old timezone
        newZone - the new timezone
